rationale: The US decision to cut air and naval assets deployed for NATO operations in Europe is a qualitatively new development that directly affects Poland's security umbrella.

top_events.title: US to cut air and naval assets deployed for NATO operations in Europe
top_events.summary: Washington is reducing military assets supporting NATO in Europe, weakening the collective defense posture that Poland relies on given its eastern border with Russia and Belarus. This is a fresh, concrete security downgrade.

top_events.title: Iran says deal to end fighting with US has 'never been closer'
top_events.summary: A US-Iran deal could ease global oil market tensions and reduce geopolitical uncertainty, indirectly benefiting Poland's energy costs and economic stability.

top_events.title: Ukraine reclaims territory as it doubles attacks on Russian logistics
top_events.summary: Ukraine's continued battlefield gains are a positive signal for Polish security, but this is qualitatively similar to the previous cycle — no new escalation toward Poland.
